name: Arkansas Am & N College
description_short: Federal credit union founded in 1952 serving University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff employees, families, and alumni with savings accounts, loans, and member-owned financial services.
description_long: Arkansas AM&N College Federal Credit Union (AM&NFCU) was established in 1952 by employees of Arkansas AM&N College (now University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff) during an era when traditional financial institutions were unwilling to extend credit to this community. The credit union emerged as a practical solution to limited access to fair lending, and it remains operational over 70 years later as a not-for-profit financial cooperative. The credit union offers multiple savings options, competitive loan products at low costs, and member-friendly benefits. Their service model reinvests member deposits back into loans and investments to generate income for operational efficiency. They provide online banking access, a loan calculator, membership applications, and loan applications through their digital platform at uapbfcu.virtualcu.net. Member savings are federally insured up to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), a U.S. government agency. AM&NFCU distinguishes itself through its member-owned cooperative structure, governance by an elected Board of Directors, and specific field of membership tied to the University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff community. The organization emphasizes quality, accountability, and professional service delivery. Their staff commitment to convenient and affordable financial solutions reflects their original mission to serve an underserved population. The credit union's scope is intentionally limited to its defined membership (current/former UAPB employees, immediate families, and alumni), which provides community focus but restricts accessibility. The website content does not specify current interest rates, fee structures beyond a reference to a fee schedule, loan limits, or details on specific account types, requiring prospective members to contact them directly or review printed materials for complete product information.
services: Online banking platform with login and account access
Loan calculator tool for payment estimation
Annual member meetings and governance participation
pros: Federally insured savings up to $250,000 by the NCUA
Member-owned cooperative structure with democratic governance by elected Board of Directors
Founded specifically to serve underserved community when mainstream banks refused credit
Online banking platform available for account access and management
Loan calculator tool for member planning and comparison
Low-cost borrowing emphasized as core member benefit
70+ year operating history demonstrating stability and community commitment
cons: Limited membership eligibility restricted to UAPB employees, families, and alumni—cannot serve general public
Website lacks specific interest rates, APR information, and detailed fee schedules; requires separate contact
No information on minimum deposit requirements, account opening processes, or specific loan products offered
Limited digital presence with no mobile app mentioned and minimal social media details
No information on available account types or their distinguishing features beyond 'several saving options'
best_for: University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff employees seeking member-owned financial institution with community roots
UAPB alumni looking to maintain financial relationships with their institutional community
Families of UAPB employees who qualify for membership and prioritize cooperative lending models
diagnosis: Best for University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff employees, alumni, and their families who are already eligible for membership and seek a member-owned cooperative with 70+ years of community commitment. The main caveat is that this credit union has restricted membership and does not serve the general public, making it only accessible to those with direct ties to UAPB.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
